creator: Julie Bort
title: (No AI) World model maker Odyssey nabs $1.45B valuation backed by Amazon and other big names
dc:creator: Julie Bort
content: World models are the next big thing in AI beyond LLMs and, with this round, Odyssey has cemented itself as one of the startups to watch.
contentSnippet: World models are the next big thing in AI beyond LLMs and, with this round, Odyssey has cemented itself as one of the startups to watch.

creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Only 16 percent of Americans think AI will have a positive impact on society, a new study shows
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: Although Wall Street loves AI, every day Americans are significantly less optimistic about the industry, a new report from Pew Research shows.
contentSnippet: Although Wall Street loves AI, every day Americans are significantly less optimistic about the industry, a new report from Pew Research shows.

title: (No AI) Two-thirds of Americans think AI is advancing too quickly
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![Thumbs down from robot symbolizing dislike of AI by the youths](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/STK_414_AI_J-copy.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

According to the [latest Pew Research poll](https://www.pewresearch.org/internet/2026/06/17/americans-and-ai-2026-chatbots-smart-devices-and-views-on-impact/), 49 percent of Americans report using chatbots at least occasionally, but 63 percent think the tech is advancing too quickly. Overall, use of AI chatbots has increased dramatically since 2024, when only 33 percent reported using them. Specifically, ChatGPT's usage has doubled since 2023, with 44 percent of respondents saying they've used it. But opinions [remain](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/644853/pew-gallup-data-americans-dont-trust-ai) [negative](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/780184/pew-research-americans-want-ai-out-of-their-personal-lives), with only 16 percent saying that AI will have a positive impact on society.

Interestingly, it's the younger generations who both report using AI more and who are inclined to have a [more pessimistic view](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/920401/gen-z-ai). 66 percent of Americans betw …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/951653/pew-research-ai-chatbot-usage-advancing-too-quickly)
contentSnippet:
According to the latest Pew Research poll, 49 percent of Americans report using chatbots at least occasionally, but 63 percent think the tech is advancing too quickly. Overall, use of AI chatbots has increased dramatically since 2024, when only 33 percent reported using them. Specifically, ChatGPT's usage has doubled since 2023, with 44 percent of respondents saying they've used it. But opinions remain negative, with only 16 percent saying that AI will have a positive impact on society.
Interestingly, it's the younger generations who both report using AI more and who are inclined to have a more pessimistic view. 66 percent of Americans betw …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: According to the latest Pew Research poll, 49 percent of Americans report using chatbots at least occasionally, but 63 percent think the tech is advancing too quickly. Overall, use of AI chatbots has increased dramatically since 2024, when only 33 percent reported using them. Specifically, ChatGPT's usage has doubled since 2023, with 44 percent of […]

title: (No AI) Vibe-decoding the White House-Anthropic fight over Fable
author: Tina Nguyen
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/gettyimages-2282033342.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

CEO of Anthropic Dario Amodei attends a working lunch with G7 leaders, G7 outreach partners, and global tech CEOs on innovation and AI, during the G7 Summit on June 17th, 2026, in Evian-les-Bains, France. | Photo: Anna Moneymaker / Getty Images

_Hello and welcome to_ Regulator_, an email for_ Verge _subscribers about technology, politics, and what happens when science crashes headlong into self-interest. Not a subscriber?_ [_Sign up here today_](https://www.theverge.com/subscribe%5D)_! Got the scoop on a petty feud that's going to somehow fundamentally reshape the entire field of frontier AI development? Send 'em over to_ [**_tina.nguyen+tips@theverge.com_**](mailto:tina.nguyen+tips@theverge.com)**_._**

Back when I was covering **Donald Trump**'s first presidency, it was incredibly common to read three different versions of the same story. His administration had split into several factions, all of which had different interests, and all of which _hated_ each other. There was the **Reince Pr …**

title: (No AI) A near-autonomous AI chemist improves a challenging reaction in medicinal chemistry
content: OpenAI and Molecule.one show how a near-autonomous AI chemist using GPT-5.4 improved a key drug-making reaction, advancing medicinal chemistry research.
contentSnippet: OpenAI and Molecule.one show how a near-autonomous AI chemist using GPT-5.4 improved a key drug-making reaction, advancing medicinal chemistry research.

title: (No AI) Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
content: OpenAI introduces Deployment Simulation, a method to predict AI model behavior before deployment using real conversation data to improve safety and evaluation accuracy.
contentSnippet: OpenAI introduces Deployment Simulation, a method to predict AI model behavior before deployment using real conversation data to improve safety and evaluation accuracy.

title: (No AI) Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning
content: UK government partners with Google DeepMind to build a new AI-powered prototype aimed at faster housing decisions.
contentSnippet: UK government partners with Google DeepMind to build a new AI-powered prototype aimed at faster housing decisions.

creator: Hugo Lowell
title: (No AI) The White House Wants Anthropic to Block All Jailbreaks. That May Not Be Possible
dc:creator: Hugo Lowell
content: Trump administration officials tell WIRED that if Anthropic wants to rerelease Fable 5, it will need to ensure the model's guardrails can't be circumvented. Security experts say that can't be done.
contentSnippet: Trump administration officials tell WIRED that if Anthropic wants to rerelease Fable 5, it will need to ensure the model's guardrails can't be circumvented. Security experts say that can't be done.

creator: Julian Chokkattu
title: (No AI) The Gemini-Powered Google Home Speaker Is Finally Here
dc:creator: Julian Chokkattu
content: Arriving six years after Google’s last smart speaker, the new HomePod-style device was redesigned to play host to Gemini’s chatbot.
contentSnippet: Arriving six years after Google’s last smart speaker, the new HomePod-style device was redesigned to play host to Gemini’s chatbot.
